scenario: A FEEL expression computes absolute value: abs(-15.5) → 15.5. The team wonders if FEEL has an abs() function.
question: Which FEEL built-in returns absolute value?

options.text: abs(n) to compute the absolute value in FEEL -- this applies JavaScript object-method syntax to a language that uses a different convention. FEEL built-in functions are called with bare names without namespace prefixes. abs(n). Using the JS-style prefix would result in a FEEL evaluation error because Math is not a recognised FEEL context or namespace in Camunda 8's engine.
abs(n) is the FEEL numeric built-in for absolute value. 5; abs(duration("-P5D")) = P5D. abs() or Abs()). md. Combine with other built-ins: floor(abs(x)) for rounded absolute value.

Write a manual conditional =if n < 0 then -n else n to compute absolute value -- this technique works correctly for number inputs but reinvents what the built-in abs(n) provides more concisely. The manual approach also requires separate handling if the input could be a duration type, whereas abs(duration("-P5D")) returns P5D directly. Using the dedicated built-in is cleaner, more readable, and handles both numbers and durations without extra logic.

FEEL has no built-in function for computing absolute value -- this is inaccurate. md as a numeric built-in that returns the absolute value of its argument. It works on numbers and duration types. FEEL has a rich set of numeric built-ins including floor, ceiling, round variants, decimal, sqrt, log, exp, modulo, odd, even, and random number, alongside abs.

a.text: Incorrect. FEEL abs(n) returns absolute value. Works on: Numbers: abs(-15.5) = 15.5; abs(0) = 0; abs(15.5) = 15.5. Days-and-time durations: abs(duration("-P5D")) = P5D. Years-and-months durations: same idea. Other Camunda FEEL numeric built-ins (verified verbatim against feel-built-in-functions-numeric.md): floor(n), floor(n, scale), ceiling(n), ceiling(n, scale): directional rounding (:29-99). round up(n, scale), round down(n, scale), round half up(n, scale), round half down(n, scale): four named rounding variants per :103-203. No round half even (Pattern #38 -- banker's rounding name fabricated). decimal(n, scale): round to specific scale per :9-27. Aggregations live in list built-ins, not numeric: min, max, mean, median, mode, stddev, sum, product per feel-built-in-functions-list.md. sqrt, exp, log, modulo: math functions per :227-294. No power built-in -- exponentiation is the operator ** per feel-numeric-expressions.md:60-67 (Pattern #54). even, odd, random number: parity + RNG per :295-336. Combine for expressions: abs(actual - expected) > tolerance (tolerance comparison), floor(abs(n)) (rounded absolute), etc.
